title: manulea tenella
intro: Manulea tenella, also known as the small-leaved manulea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues, such as colds and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Manulea tenella include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve coughs, and aid in the recovery from respiratory infections. Traditionally, Manulea tenella is prepared as a tea by infusing the dried leaves and flowers in hot water, or used as a tincture by mixing the plant extract with a solvent like ethanol.
